name: Duskhollow — A Fair Day Turns (demo)
dmNotes: Showcase scene, fully Space-driven. Three beats: sunny town → nightfall & storm → eerie shrine. Press Space to walk every cue in order — music crossfades, one-shot SFX, and the ambient beds (crowd/wind/rain/dripping/temple/ghostly), which are SUSTAINED LOOPING SFX: they fade in on their cue and keep looping until their cue fires again (crowd cuts at 'stalls empty'; rain & wind cut at the shrine door). Weather + cave beds are pro field recordings (Nox_Sound). All CC0.

ideas.text: The coin-seller knows the shrine's name and won't say it aloud
The heartbeat is faster when a character lies

scriptText:
# Duskhollow — A Fair Day Turns

## Market Square, Midday

{{music:town}} The morning bells have long since faded, and Duskhollow's market square {{sfx:crowd}} glows under a flawless sky. Awnings snap in a warm breeze, a fiddler saws out a jig, and the smell of fresh bread drifts between the stalls. A coin-seller counts his take {{sfx:coins}} and waves you toward his trinkets. *It is, by every measure, a beautiful day.*

> [!dm] Space starts the fair-day theme, then the market crowd bed, then the coin clink. Let them breathe here — this is the calm before.

## Dusk, and the Rain

But the light is going wrong. {{music:storm}} Clouds the color of old bruises pile over the eastern hills, and a cold wind rises {{sfx:wind}} that tugs at cloaks and strangles the fiddler's tune. The first fat drops strike the cobbles, thunder rolls across the rooftops {{sfx:thunder}} — then the sky opens {{sfx:rain}}. Stalls empty, shutters slam {{sfx:crowd}}. One of you strikes a lantern against the gloom {{sfx:torch}}, and its little flame is the only warm thing left in Duskhollow.

> [!dm] The wind and rain loops fade in on Space; the second crowd cue at "stalls empty" shuts the crowd off. Sustained beds keep looping until fired again.

## The Sunless Shrine

The road out of town ends at a door that should not be here — black stone, older than Duskhollow itself. It groans inward at a touch {{sfx:door}}, and the storm cuts off behind you {{sfx:rain}} {{sfx:wind}}. Inside, the air is cold and still and *wrong*. {{music:temple}} Water drips somewhere in the dark {{sfx:dripping}}, and the shrine itself seems to hold its breath {{sfx:templebed}}. Something breathes that has no business breathing {{sfx:ghost}}, and the shadows will not stay still {{sfx:ghostly}}. Far below, beneath the altar, you feel a slow, patient pulse — like a heart the size of a room {{sfx:heart}}. **You are not alone in here.** {{sfx:spell}}

> [!dm] The rain and wind chips at the door fire those loops a second time to cut the storm as they step inside. Then dripping and the temple bed come in, then the ghostly loop. Save the dark-magic sting for the last line.
